commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] The remaining action is maintainer acceptance of the local /status command contract, not a narrow automated repair.; Cleared: The diff does not touch dependencies, CI, credentials, auth, filesystem permissions, or package execution surfaces, and it reduces accidental model exposure of known local control commands. (sha=59a12db407e8e54667f1d48c7b8f901cf2cacf65)
commands.review_summary:
The branch updates local TUI slash parsing, discovery/help, command handling, and PTY/unit coverage so known local or unsupported shared slash commands are handled locally or rejected instead of sent to the embedded model, with a few formatting-only conflict-resolution hunks outside TUI.

PR surface: Source +119, Tests +284. Total +403 across 9 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main advertises shared TUI commands, lacks local `status`/`compact` cases, and falls through to `sendMessage(raw)` in local mode. I did not run the PTY lane because this review is read-only, but the path is clear in source and the PR supplies PTY proof.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining action is maintainer acceptance of the local `/status` command contract, not a narrow automated repair.

target.title: fix(tui): keep local slash commands out of model prompts
target.body:
Closes #71592.

## Summary

- Keep known local embedded TUI control commands out of the model path.
- Render local `/status` from TUI session state.
- Report known shared commands that local embedded mode cannot run, such as `/compact`, `/context`, and `/commands all`, as unsupported instead of forwarding them.
- Keep local embedded help/autocomplete from advertising unsupported shared commands such as `/commands`, `/compact`, and `/context`.
- Keep local `/stop` discoverable and local when there is no active embedded run.
- Preserve `/btw` and `/side` side-question behavior, directive-only aliases such as `/t ...` and `/reason ...`, active-run `/stop`, and unknown slash commands as model/directive prompts.

## AI assistance disclosure

This PR was implemented with Codex assistance. I reviewed the change and understand the affected local TUI command path.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: local embedded TUI slash commands no longer fall through to the model when the TUI can handle them locally or knows they are unsupported shared control commands.

Real environment tested: local `openclaw tui --local` PTY run with isolated HOME/config/state/workspace and a mocked OpenAI-compatible model endpoint. No live credentials were used.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
OPENCLAW_TUI_PTY_INCLUDE_LOCAL=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.tui-pty.config.ts src/tui/tui-pty-local.e2e.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix:

```text
RUN  v4.1.7 <worktree>

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       2 passed (2)
Duration    15.11s
```

Observed result after fix: the PTY proof starts `tui --local`, types `/status`, `/compact: focus on decisions`, and `/commands all`, and verifies the mock model endpoint receives zero requests for those commands. It then types `/not-a-real-command` and verifies the model endpoint receives one request and the TUI displays `LOCAL_PTY_RESPONSE`.

What was not tested: live provider credentials, gateway mode TUI behavior, and a real interactive terminal recording outside the PTY harness.

## Root Cause

The local TUI autocomplete list already included shared chat commands from the command registry, but `handleCommand` only implemented a subset of those names. In local embedded mode, unsupported shared commands could hit the default branch and call `sendMessage(raw)`, which hands the text to the embedded run.

The TUI parser also did not match the shared registry's colon-form command shape, so inputs such as `/compact: ...` could route differently from the registry contract. After the rebase, local `/goal: ...` needed the same normalization before calling the embedded goal handler.

## Dependency contract

This depends on OpenClaw's internal shared command registry, not an external package. `src/auto-reply/commands-registry-normalize.ts` already normalizes `/command: args` to `/command args` and resolves text aliases through the registry. `src/auto-reply/commands-registry.data.ts` rebuilds command metadata when the active channel/plugin registry version changes.

The TUI now mirrors the command-token part of that contract before deciding whether local embedded mode should handle, reject, or forward a slash input. The derived shared-command token map is rebuilt from `listChatCommands()` when needed so it does not hold a stale TUI-local cache. Directive-only aliases such as `/t`, `/thinking`, `/v`, and `/reason` stay on the directive path instead of being converted into local TUI setting commands. Local `/goal: ...` is normalized to `/goal ...` before calling `runGoalCommand`.

## Regression Test Plan

Focused tests:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tui/tui-command-handlers.test.ts src/tui/commands.test.ts
```

Result:

```text
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests       78 passed (78)
```

This includes local discovery coverage for `/stop`, local idle `/stop` behavior, colon-form shared commands, directive-only aliases, unsupported shared commands, and unknown slash forwarding.

Real-path PTY proof:

```bash
OPENCLAW_TUI_PTY_INCLUDE_LOCAL=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.tui-pty.config.ts src/tui/tui-pty-local.e2e.test.ts
```

Result:

```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       2 passed (2)
```

Changed gate:

```bash
OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree pnpm check:changed
```

Result:

```text
[check:changed] lanes=core, coreTests
typecheck core passed
typecheck core tests passed
lint core changed files passed
runtime import cycles: 0 runtime value cycle(s)
```

## Security Impact

Low direct security impact. The change reduces accidental model exposure of local control-command text in embedded TUI mode. It does not touch auth, secrets, provider credentials, filesystem access, config, or plugin loading.

## Human Verification

I checked the runtime path from TUI submit through `createCommandHandlers`, `EmbeddedTuiBackend.sendChat`, and the shared command registry. The local PTY proof uses isolated state and a local mock endpoint so the result is reproducible without secrets.

## CODEOWNERS / owner review

No CODEOWNERS rule matches `src/tui/**`. TUI maintainer review is welcome, but there is no CODEOWNERS-required owner for this patch.

## Changelog

No `CHANGELOG.md` edit. This is a contributor PR; release-note context is in this PR body.

## Review conversations

- `autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main --model gpt-5.3-codex-spark`: clean after rebase, no accepted/actionable findings.
- `codex review --base origin/main`: first reported a stale TUI-local shared-command alias cache. Fixed by rebuilding the derived map from `listChatCommands()` instead of caching it in the TUI handler.
- `codex review --base origin/main`: then reported directive alias interception for `/t ...`, `/thinking ...`, `/v ...`, and `/reason ...`. Fixed by preserving those aliases for the inline directive path.
- ClawSweeper initially rated the draft PR `gold shrimp` because the branch was behind current `main`. I rebased onto current `upstream/main`, preserved the newer TUI run/session bookkeeping, and reran the focused tests, local PTY proof, changed gate, autoreview, and Codex review.
- Follow-up `codex -m gpt-5.3-codex-spark review --base origin/main` reported `/goal: ...` normalization and local `/help` advertising `/commands`. Fixed both with focused tests.
- ClawSweeper then found local `/stop` was handled but no longer discoverable in the local embedded command list. Fixed by adding `stop` to the local shared-command discovery allowlist and focused parser coverage.
- Final `codex -m gpt-5.3-codex-spark review --base origin/main`: clean, no concrete regression found in the changed local or gateway TUI paths. The review also reran the focused TUI parser, handler, and PTY lanes during inspection.
- Final ClawSweeper review after the rebased push: 🦞 diamond lobster, proof sufficient, ready for maintainer look. The only remaining note is maintainer acceptance of the compact local `/status` behavior listed below.

## Risks

- Local `/status` now shows a compact TUI status summary, not the full shared chat `/status` report.

## Behavior tradeoffs

- Known but unsupported shared commands now produce a local system line instead of being sent to the model.
- Unsupported shared commands are no longer advertised by local embedded discovery/help, but manually typed unsupported commands still get an explicit local message.
- Directive-only aliases with prompts stay on the agent/directive path, so `/t high explain the diff` keeps its existing inline behavior.
- Unknown slash commands still forward as prompts, preserving the old escape hatch for user-defined or future model-facing slash text.

## Scope boundaries

- No change to gateway-mode TUI behavior.
- No embedded-mode implementation for `/compact` or `/context`.
- No shared command registry rewrite.
- No config, migration, plugin, or provider surface change.

## Validation

Completed:

```text
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/tui/commands.ts src/tui/commands.test.ts src/tui/tui-command-handlers.ts src/tui/tui-command-handlers.test.ts src/tui/tui-pty-local.e2e.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tui/tui-command-handlers.test.ts src/tui/commands.test.ts
OPENCLAW_TUI_PTY_INCLUDE_LOCAL=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.tui-pty.config.ts src/tui/tui-pty-local.e2e.test.ts
OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree pnpm check:changed
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main --model gpt-5.3-codex-spark ...
codex -m gpt-5.3-codex-spark review --base origin/main
```

Current synced base for this branch is `upstream/main` / `origin/main` at `60104fe254805c3ff78c532d5bbe82d67032b924`.
target.author: ferminquant

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
